content.full_text:
CITY OF COSTA MESA
CALIFORNIA 92628-1200

FROM THE OFFICE OF THE CITY ATTORNEY

October 25, 1991


Scott Hallabrian
Acting General Counsel
Legal Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Re: Advice Letter Concerning Private Employment
    of Sharon Buffa, the Spouse of Peter Buffa,
    Chairman of the Costa Mesa Redevelopment Agency
    and City of Costa Mesa City Council Member

Dear Mr. Hallabrian:

     Pursuant to a request of City of Costa Mesa City Council
Member Peter Buffa, this office requests that the Fair Political
Practices Commission ("F.P.P.C.") provide written advice with
respect to Mr. Buffa's duties under the Political Reform Act of
1974, as amended ("Act"), and California Government Code ("G.C.")
Section 1090.

     The questions presented are as follows:

     1)    Will Mr. Buffa, Chairman of the Costa Mesa
Redevelopment Agency, violate G.C. Section 1090 when he votes on
matters affecting Triangle Square Joint Venture, a developer of
redevelopment property under a development agreement with the
Costa Mesa Redevelopment Agency, where the developer employs the
principle officer's, as individuals, of a public relations
corporation in which Sharon Buffa is an employee?

     2)    Will Mr. Buffa, as a Council Member or Agency Chairman,
be disqualified from voting on all matters directly involving or
affecting Triangle Square Joint Venture due to Sharon Buffa's
employment with a public relations corporation where the
principle officers, as individuals, are to be employed by such
developer?



77 FAIR DRIVE  •  (714) 754-5399  •  FAX: (714) 556-7508

Scott Hallabrian
October 25, 1991
Page Two


     The pertinent facts related by Mr. Buffa to this office are
as follows:

     Peter Buffa is a City of Costa Mesa ("City") Council Member
and Chairman of the Costa Mesa Redevelopment Agency ("Agency").
While in such capacity, the Agency approved a development
agreement with Triangle Square Joint Venture ("TSJV") to
participate in the development of a parcel of property owned by
the Agency as part of a redevelopment plan for the downtown area
of the City.  The TSJV project consists of a shopping mall and
the mall is very near completion.

     Sharon Buffa is the wife of Mr. Buffa.  Since the summer of
1991, Mrs. Buffa has been employed as a consultant by a
corporation known as the Greensburqh Group, Inc..  The
corporation was formed in early 1991 by Claudia Roxburgh and Glen
Greener, who are the officers and shareholders.  Mr. Roxburgh is
the principal owner of a public relations/advertising agency
known as the Roxburgh Agency.  Mr. Greener is the principal owner
of a political consulting/public relations agency known as
Greener & Adams.

     The Roxburgh Agency and Greener & Adams are separate
business entities with separate bank accounts; however, the two
agencies share a floor of offices but each agency pays their own
share of the leasing expenses.  The Greenburgh Group, Inc., is a
corporation and a separate business from the Roxburgh Agency and
Green & Adams.  The Greenburgh Group, Inc., on special projects
has purchased various services from the Roxburgh Agency such as
art direction, layout, typesetting, etc., at favorable rates.

     In October, 1991, the Roxburgh Agency expressed interest in
obtaining an agreement with TSJV to provide public relations/
advertising services for the shopping mall.  The proposed
agreement would retain the Roxburgh Agency and Mr. Greener as an
individual.  Neither the Greenburgh Group, Inc. nor Ms. Buffa
would be employed by or perform services for the shopping mall
for the Roxburgh Agency, Mr. Greener or TSJV.

     Given the complexity of the above-referenced businesses,
this office has recommended that written advice be obtained from
the F.P.P.C.  Mr. Greener and the Roxburgh Agency and the
officers of TSJV have agreed to not enter into the agreement for
public relations/advertising services pending the written advice
from the F.P.P.C. concerning Council Member/Chairman Buffa's
duties under the Act and G.C. Section 1090.

Scott Hallabrian
October 25, 1991
Page Three


     Therefore, based on the above-referenced facts, this office
requests, on behalf of Council Member/Chairman Buffa, a written
advice letter on his duties under the Act and G.C. Section 1090
pursuant to Government Code Section 83114(b).

     If there are any questions, please contact this office.
                                        Very truly yours,


                                        Thomas A. Kathe
                                        City Attorney

TAK:pb

cc:  Council Member Peter Buffa
